brief: GAP/WHY: Thermal ablation energies (RF, cryo) are standard for PVI in paroxysmal AF and considered effective/safe, but long-term success is limited and complications arise from thermal spread into non-target tissues; PFA is non-thermal and tissue-selective, potentially safer/more effective but lacks head-to-head RCT evidence vs RF in drug-resistant paroxysmal AF. DESIGN/SETTING: This is a design/rationale paper for the BEAT PAROX-AF trial — a European multicentre, superiority, open-label, randomized controlled trial; two parallel arms; 9 high-volume European clinical centres across 5 countries. POPULATION/N: 292 participants recruited; patients with drug-resistant (drug-refractory) paroxysmal AF. INTERVENTION vs COMPARISON: PFA arm using FARAPULSE Endocardial Ablation System (Boston Scientific) vs RF ablation arm using the CLOSE protocol with a contact-force-sensing catheter (SmartTouch catheter, CARTO mapping, Biosense Webster); 1:1-style randomization to the two arms. ENDPOINTS: Primary endpoint = 1-year recurrence of atrial arrhythmia. Major secondary safety endpoint = occurrence of acute (<7 days) procedure-related serious adverse events, OR pulmonary vein stenosis, OR atrio-oesophageal fistula up to 12 months. SUB-STUDIES (five): (1) oesophageal safety, (2) cerebral lesions, (3) cardiac autonomic nervous system effects, (4) durability of PVI assessed during redo/repeat ablation procedures, (5) atrial and ventricular function. TIMELINE: Study started 27 December 2021; recruitment concluded 17 January 2024; results expected mid-2025. TAKEAWAY: Trial is designed to determine whether PFA is superior to RF ablation in efficacy and safety for drug-resistant paroxysmal AF, providing insight into the optimal treatment approach. NOTE: This is a trial protocol/rationale — no outcome results are reported (no effect sizes, p-values, or CIs available).
